title: First Time Setup: Environment → Calibration → Teleop
description: Complete first-run flow for a new Solo CLI user. Covers env setup, install, motor configuration, calibration, and first teleop session.

detect_os.instruction: What operating system are you on?

install_uv_mac.instruction: Run this to install the uv package manager. It's fast and handles Python versions automatically.

install_uv_linux.instruction: Run this to install uv on Linux.

setup_usb_linux.instruction: On Linux, you need to grant serial port access for the robot arm. Run this once and then log out and back in.

install_uv_windows.instruction: Run this in PowerShell (as admin if needed).

create_venv.instruction: Create an isolated Python 3.12 environment. This keeps solo-cli dependencies separate from the rest of your system.

on_failure.recovery: uv will auto-fetch it — try again and wait for the download

activate_venv.instruction: Activate the venv. Your terminal prompt should show (.venv) after this.
activate_venv.validation_prompt: Do you see (.venv) at the start of your terminal prompt?

on_failure.recovery: Ensure you're in the same directory where you ran uv venv. Try: ls .venv/

install_solo_cli.instruction: How do you want to install solo-cli? Recommended: GitHub source install (gets you the latest code and lets you stay up to date with pulls).

install_from_source.instruction:
Clone solo-cli from GitHub and install in editable mode. Run: git clone https://github.com/GetSoloTech/solo-cli.git && cd solo-cli && uv pip install -e .

Editable mode means updates are as simple as `git pull` — no reinstall needed.

install_mac_script.instruction: Run the Mac automated installer: ./install_mac.sh — this clones from GitHub and installs from source in editable mode automatically.

install_from_pypi.instruction:
Install solo-cli from PyPI: uv pip install solo-cli

Note: this installs a release snapshot. For the latest code, use the GitHub source method instead.

verify_install.instruction: Let's confirm solo-cli is installed. Run: solo --help

validation.rule: Output must list commands including: setup, robo, serve, status, list, test, stop, download, setup-usb

on_failure.recovery: activate_venv — solo may not be in PATH if venv is inactive

check_arm_motors.instruction: Did your arm come pre-configured from Solo Tech? (Most do)

try_calibrate_first.instruction: Open terminal popup with scope='all'. If the terminal output shows a 'missing motor ids' error, proceed to setup_motors_all. Otherwise confirm success.

setup_motors_all.instruction: Open terminal popup immediately (no pre-flight needed — scope is 'all'). Tell user the terminal will ask them to unplug and replug each arm for port detection — follow prompts exactly.

scan_motors_diagnostic.instruction: Let's scan what's visible on serial ports to diagnose the issue.

on_failure.message: Check: USB cable to arm, power supply voltage (12V follower, 5V leader), and all 3-pin motor cables are seated

calibrate_all.instruction: Open terminal popup immediately (scope is 'all', no pre-flight needed). Tell user the terminal will ask them to move each joint to its limit and press Enter — keep workspace clear and move slowly.

on_failure.message: Can re-run calibration for just the arm that failed

calibrate_leader_only.instruction: Open terminal popup with scope='leader'. Tell user to follow on-screen joint movement prompts.

on_failure.message: If calibration fails again, run scan_motors via Shell tool to check hardware

calibrate_follower_only.instruction: Open terminal popup with scope='follower'. Tell user to follow on-screen joint movement prompts.

on_failure.message: If calibration fails again, run scan_motors via Shell tool to check hardware

teleop_ready.instruction: Apply PRE-FLIGHT PROTOCOL: if robot type, leader arm ID, or follower arm ID are unknown, ask all of them in ONE message. Check ~/.solo/ via Shell tool if arm IDs are unclear. Then construct the full teleop command with all params as flags and open terminal popup. Tell user: move the leader arm and the follower will mirror it — press Ctrl+C in the terminal to stop.
teleop_ready.validation_prompt: Does the follower arm move when you move the leader?

on_failure.message: Tool auto-retries port detection once. Executor will run solo robo --scan if still failing.

tutorial_complete.instruction: You've completed the first-run setup. You can now record datasets with: solo robo --record
